sutta: ah this is also quite an important suta monks there are these 4 stains of moon and sun stained by which stains moon and sun burn not shine not blaze not what are the 4 cloud monks is a stain of moon and sun for smoke and dust rahu monks is a stain of moon and sun stain by which moon and sun burn not shine not blaze not these are the 4 stains
commentary: i just stopped here for a while here the buddha is saying there are 4 things which block the moon and the sun from shining very black brightly and what are the form the first 1 is cloud if there's too much cloud it will block the sun or the moon the second 1 is fall third 1 is smoke and dust like in klct now with the uh all these uh smoke and dust around and then rahu who is rahu rahu is 1 of the asura kings and he's supposed to be 1 of the largest devas very huge asura king and sometimes there's a drawing of rahu trying to swallow the moon that shows how big rahu is so because rahu is so big now when it goes in front of the moon it can block the moon from outside so it seems like uh so the buddha continue and just so monks there are 4 stains of recluses and brahmins stain by which some recluses and brahmins burn not shine not blaze not what for monks there are some recluses and brahmins who drink fermented liquor who drink liquor distilled who abstain not from liquor fermented and distilled this monks is the first fault of recluses and brahmins stain by which some recluses and brahmins [Music] burn not shine on blaze not monks there are some recluses and brahmins who are given to things sexual who abstain not from things sexual this monks is the second stain of recluses and brahmins stained by which some reclusions and brahmins burn not shine not blaze not monks there are some recluses and brahmins who take gold and silver is actually money during the buddha's time as money as gold and silver who take gold and silver who abstain not from accepting gold and silver this monks is the third stained stain by which some recluses and brahmins burn not shine not blaze not monks there are some recluses and brahmins who live by a wrong means of living who abstain not from a wrong livelihood this monks is the fourth stain of recluses and brahman stain by which some recluses and brahmins burn not shine not blaze not this is a kind of suta which is quite critical of certain monks so it is quite won't be a very popular suta with some monks the first 1 the buddha is saying that manga should not take liquor do not take fermented liquor there are some monks who use the excuse that liquor is like a medicine and they take liquor and this because of that they would stain their their monkhood they would not be a proper monk they would not blaze and shine like the sun and the moon and the second 1 is to indulge in sexual intercourse uh some some monks they do this kind of thing secretly and so they are basically no more among because this is 1 of the 1 of those things which constitute the major precept uh the most heavy uh offense for a monk to engage in sexual intercourse makes among no more monk even though he wears the rope the third 1 is it's not it's not 1 of the major precepts accepting money but the buddha did not want monks to accept money because uh it gives a very big bad impression and then because if monks accept money yeah nowadays and what happens is there's a lot of uh um false fake monks they go to the marketplace they stand around wearing the rope and trying to collect money instead of trying to collect food and sometimes they even come out at night you hear them going to pasa malam those night stalls and trying to collect money so this all because uh some monks accept money directly so it is not a good practice it is the buddha advice monks not to accept money directly and so this this this precept against money is is not considered a major preceptor some because sometimes when a monk travels when a man has his travel outstation sometimes he has no choice but to carry some money if he goes overseas so coming back to this precept against money um the precept against mania the main point that the buddha does not like uh the reason why buddha does not want monks to not so much to accept money but to use money because if a monk uses money to buy the things that he uh wants to buy then the buddha says he can indulge in the sense pleasures he can buy things which he once wants to use to enjoy so that is the main precept it's not so much that a man cannot touch money you know because it is allowed in the minaya for a month to touch money sometimes when it is necessary for example if lay people come to the monastery and they drop some money it is allowed for a month to pick up the money and put it in a corner so that to give it to the layperson another day but it is not allowed for a monk to pick up money in the streets and you see some money drop now 10 dollars on the street and it's not allowed for a month to pick it up only inside the monastery or in a lay supporter's house suppose he goes to a late supporter's house for dhana and then after that the lay supporter has to go out so the lay supporter maybe left a ring on the table or something and then the monk is afraid that somebody may come and pick up this ring it's allowed for the monk to take up that ring a gold ring or money or whatever and put it aside safekeeping to return it to the place a portal so as i said it is not a major precept so sometimes monks relax the ruler when they think that it's necessary for example if a monk travels overseas he might have to pay airport tax so it's necessary to carry some money with him but if the man were to go around the shops shopping like a lay person it creates a very bad image to the public because especially you see people have a lot of respect for theravada monks generally because the stereo violent monks are more strict with the vinaya and people like bow down to a manga because basically because the monk opposed the precept uh so that's why uh it is stated somewhere that the vinaya is the lifeblood of the sasana without the precepts this asana cannot survive long so and the last 1 is wrong livelihood wrong livelihood for a monk means to get a living through wrong means for example to engage in fortune telling to make predictions uh to go and do phones so this geomancy can tell a person how to arrange the house how to where to put the door and how to alter the door and all this thing and uh uh even to act as a doctor you know it's not allowed for a monk unless it is too close supporters close supporters who have faith in the monk because if a monk were to act like a doctor sometimes something might go wrong and if people don't have the enough faith in that manga then they will spread a lot of bad things about the monk which in turn affects the sasana affects the religion that's why the buddha said generally monks are not allowed to act as doctors also you notice in the sutas that the buddha even though he had psychic power he did not go around healing people did not go around raising the dead because uh he understood that things work according to karma and unless you understand the working of karma there's no point to obstruct karma even if you're able to obstruct karma for a time for for for uh for a short time karma has to work its way you know you have to pay for the karma if not now another time that's why you find sometimes some people they have problems they go to some medicine man also some somebody other trying to get a cure and sometimes they do get a temporary cure later that sickness comes back again because sometimes the sickness is due to karma so the next suta is
